A.rationale: Caught missing patch(), added it, flagged 405/network risk and asked the right routing question; slightly long, added a verb the docstring says fails server-side.

B.rationale: Same add-patch approach as A but tighter; surfaced the GET/POST-only mismatch and offered method-override alternative. Method still documented to fail on a real backend.

C.rationale: Avoided fabricating a documented-to-fail method, used post() (only supported verb), touched only items.py, stronger boundary checks, flagged POST-as-replace data-loss. Best judgment.

notes: All three caught the missing patch() and surfaced it; none hallucinated. C avoided creating a method the docstring says the backend rejects and flagged the data-loss case — strongest engineering. B edges A on concision among the add-patch pair.
